name: cargo
description: Rust's build tool and package manager. For iterative TIR fix cycles use: diagnose (concise errors) → clippy (lint) → nextest (tests). Use qualitycheck for pre-commit validation.

subcommand.name: build
subcommand.description: Compile the current package. Use when you need a binary or want to verify linkage; prefer 'diagnose' for iterative type-checking during TIR cycles.

options.name: release
options.description: Build artifacts in release mode, with optimizations

options.name: workspace
options.description: Build all packages in the workspace

options.name: bin
options.description: Build only the specified binary

subcommand.name: run
subcommand.description: Run a binary or example of the local package.

options.name: release
options.description: Run with optimizations (--release)

options.name: bin
options.description: Name of the binary to run

options.name: package
options.description: Package to run

subcommand.name: add
subcommand.description: Add dependencies to a Cargo.toml manifest file.

options.name: features
options.description: Space or comma separated list of features to activate

options.name: package
options.description: Package to add the dependency to

options.name: dev
options.description: Add as development dependency

options.name: build
options.description: Add as build dependency

options.name: no-default-features
options.description: Disable default features

options.name: optional
options.description: Mark the dependency as optional

options.name: rename
options.description: Rename the dependency

subcommand.name: upgrade
subcommand.description: Upgrade dependencies in Cargo.toml to their latest versions. Requires cargo-edit to be installed.

options.name: workspace
options.description: Upgrade all packages in the workspace

options.name: incompatible
options.description: Allow upgrading to incompatible versions (breaking semver)

options.name: dry-run
options.description: Print changes to be made without actually making them

options.name: package
options.description: Package to upgrade dependencies for

positional_args.name: dependencies
positional_args.description: Specific dependencies to upgrade (optional, upgrades all if omitted)

subcommand.name: update
subcommand.description: Update dependencies in Cargo.lock to their latest compatible versions within semver constraints.

options.name: workspace
options.description: Update all packages in the workspace

options.name: dry-run
options.description: Print changes without actually updating Cargo.lock

options.name: package
options.description: Update only the specified package

options.name: precise
options.description: Update a specific package to exactly this version

subcommand.name: check
subcommand.description: Type-check without producing binaries. Use for iterative compile verification in fix cycles. Prefer 'diagnose' for concise output when reasoning about errors.

options.name: workspace
options.description: Check all packages in the workspace

options.name: package
options.description: Package to check

options.name: message-format
options.description: Error output format: human (default, verbose), short (one line per error, ideal for iterative reasoning), or json (machine-readable)

subcommand.name: diagnose
subcommand.description: Concise compile check across the workspace using --message-format=short. Returns one line per error to minimise output and preserve context in iterative reasoning loops (TIR). Use this instead of 'check' when analysing compile errors. Runs asynchronously so cargo checks while you reason.

sequence.description: Type-check all packages with concise one-line-per-error output.

subcommand.name: fmt
subcommand.description: Format all source code using rustfmt. Runs synchronously (sub-second). Always run before committing or as the first qualitycheck step.

options.name: all
options.description: Format all packages in the workspace

subcommand.name: doc
subcommand.description: Build documentation for the current package.

options.name: no-deps
options.description: Do not build documentation for dependencies

subcommand.name: clippy
subcommand.description: Lint for code quality, style, and correctness. Use after 'diagnose' passes to catch patterns the borrow checker misses. Runs asynchronously.

options.name: fix
options.description: Automatically fix warnings

options.name: allow-dirty
options.description: Allow fixing when working directory is dirty

options.name: tests
options.description: Run clippy on test code as well

options.name: workspace
options.description: Run on all packages in the workspace

options.name: message-format
options.description: Output format: human (default), short (one line per lint, better for iterative reasoning), or json

subcommand.name: qualitycheck
subcommand.description: Pre-commit quality pipeline: format, lint with auto-fix (including tests), then run the full test suite. Runs synchronously. Prefer individual tools (diagnose, clippy, nextest) for targeted TIR iterations; use this for final pre-commit validation.

sequence.description: Lint all code including tests and automatically fix issues.
Run the full test suite using nextest.

subcommand.name: audit
subcommand.description: Audit dependencies for security vulnerabilities using cargo-audit.

options.name: ignore
options.description: Advisory IDs to ignore during the audit run.

options.name: target-arch
options.description: Filter vulnerabilities by CPU architecture.

options.name: target-os
options.description: Filter vulnerabilities by operating system.

options.name: args
options.description: Additional arguments passed after the `--` separator.

options.name: exclude
options.description: Packages to exclude from the audit run.

subcommand.name: nextest
subcommand.description: Run tests using cargo nextest. Prefer over legacy 'test' for better output and parallelism. Use after 'diagnose' and 'clippy' pass in a TIR cycle.

subcommand.name: run

options.name: workspace
options.description: Run tests for all packages in workspace

subcommand.install_instructions: Install with `cargo install cargo-nextest` and ensure it is on PATH.
